name: getOrchestrator
description:
Starts a synchronous orchestrator for complex multi-step tasks. The orchestrator runs in its own isolated AI context and has access to the configured specialist execution tool (default: getSpecialists) to launch parallel specialist workers. Blocks until the orchestrator has fully completed and returns its final result.

USE getOrchestrator when the task has ANY of these characteristics:
- Multi-step: requires multiple sequential or parallel tool calls to complete
- High complexity: needs decomposition into specialized sub-tasks
- High parallelizability: contains multiple independent sub-tasks that benefit from concurrent execution
- History/context retrieval beyond the visible context window

DO NOT use getOrchestrator for simple tasks:
- Single-tool lookups (one search, one API call, one file read)
- Direct factual answers the visible context already supports
- Short serialized tasks with one or two steps that a single tool handles

IMPORTANT: For multi-step execution tasks, do not skip directly to execution. Use plan mode first, show the returned PLAN to the user, and wait for explicit approval in a later user message.

Modes:
- mode="plan": return only a concrete execution plan for user review. Do not execute the work yet.
- mode="execute": execute the approved plan. mode="execute" is valid only when the exact approved plan text is passed in approvedPlan.

Type guide:
- type="context": Use when the question is about past events, decisions, characters, places, or anything from the conversation history not visible in the current context window. The context orchestrator reads the full conversation history with getHistory to retrieve the actual messages.
policyHint: For complex execution tasks with side effects or substantial work, first call this tool with mode=plan so the user can review the plan. After a short approval like 'yes', 'ja', 'ok', 'okay', 'mach', or 'go ahead' in a later user turn, call it again with mode=execute and pass the approved plan text back in approvedPlan. Never call mode=execute in the same turn that produced the plan. Never call mode=execute without approvedPlan. For specific factual questions or history retrieval beyond the visible context window, call this tool immediately with the appropriate type.

prompt.description: The task or goal for the orchestrator to accomplish. Be specific and include all relevant context.

mode.description: Execution mode: 'plan' returns only a plan for approval, 'execute' carries out previously approved work. Do not use 'execute' without approvedPlan.

approvedPlan.description: The exact plan text previously shown to and approved by the user. Required when mode='execute'.

type.description: The orchestrator type to use. Currently supported: 'context' (reads conversation history). If omitted, the configured defaultType is used.
